name: Grilled Walnut-Stuffed Turkey Rolls
recipeIngredient: 8 Boneless Skinless Turkey Breast Slices (1 1/2-1 3/4 Lbs.)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a nonreactive bowl, stir together the yogurt, lemon juice, and chopped chives. Set aside until serving. If refrigerating is necessary due to waiting time exceeding one hour.
2. Heat olive oil in a large skillet. Add onions and cook until soft, about 5 minutes.
3. Place thawed spinach in a strainer; press firmly to remove excess liquid. Return the spinach to the skillet and cook for an additional 3 minutes.
4. Transfer the mixture (onions, spinach) to a large bowl. Stir in bread crumbs, chopped walnuts, fresh thyme, chicken broth, salt, and pepper until combined.
5. Arrange turkey breast slices on a work surface. Lightly season with salt and pepper.
6. Divide the spinach mixture evenly among the turkey slices, spreading it over the top half of each slice.
7. Starting from the filled end, roll up each slice into a log shape.
8. Coat each turkey roll lightly with no-stick cooking spray.
9. Preheat the grill to hot coals. Grill the rolls 4-6 inches from the heat for about 15 minutes, turning several times until well browned and the turkey is fully cooked.
10. Place the rolls on a platter. They are also excellent served cold. Spoon some yogurt sauce over the tops and garnish with parsley. Serve any remaining sauce at the table.
Per serving: 376 calories, 16 grams fat, 37% of calories from fat.
